trans-unit.note: {0} is the AppHost Aspire.Hosting version. {1} is the AppHost project path.
{0} is the Aspire channel name (e.g., "stable", "staging", "daily", "local", "pr-1234"). Appended to CLI / AppHost version messages.

trans-unit.target: 正在檢查 Aspire 環境...

trans-unit.note: {0} is the current Aspire CLI version.
{0} is the command to update Aspire CLI.

trans-unit.source: Aspire CLI version {0} is out of date. Latest version is {1}
trans-unit.target: Aspire CLI version {0} is out of date. Latest version is {1}
trans-unit.note: {0} is the current Aspire CLI version. {1} is the latest available Aspire CLI version.

trans-unit.source: Could not check for Aspire CLI updates
trans-unit.target: Could not check for Aspire CLI updates

trans-unit.source: Diagnose Aspire environment issues and verify setup
trans-unit.target: 診斷 Aspire 環境問題並驗證設定

trans-unit.source: Run '{0}' to remove all certificates, then run '{1}' to create and trust a new one.
trans-unit.target: Run '{0}' to remove all certificates, then run '{1}' to create and trust a new one.
trans-unit.note: {0} is the aspire certs clean command, {1} is the aspire certs trust command (not localizable)

trans-unit.source: Found certificates: {0}. Having multiple certificates can cause confusion.
trans-unit.target: Found certificates: {0}. Having multiple certificates can cause confusion.

trans-unit.source: Multiple HTTPS development certificates found ({0} certificates), but none are trusted
trans-unit.target: Multiple HTTPS development certificates found ({0} certificates), but none are trusted

trans-unit.source: Found certificates: {0}. Having multiple certificates can cause confusion when selecting which one to use.
trans-unit.target: Found certificates: {0}. Having multiple certificates can cause confusion when selecting which one to use.

trans-unit.source: Multiple HTTPS development certificates found ({0} certificates)
trans-unit.target: Multiple HTTPS development certificates found ({0} certificates)

trans-unit.source: Aspire uses HTTPS for secure communication between the dashboard and your services during local development.
trans-unit.target: Aspire uses HTTPS for secure communication between the dashboard and your services during local development.

trans-unit.source: Certificate {0} exists in the personal store but was not found in the trusted root store.
trans-unit.target: Certificate {0} exists in the personal store but was not found in the trusted root store.

trans-unit.source: HTTPS development certificate is not trusted
trans-unit.target: HTTPS development certificate is not trusted

trans-unit.source: Older certificate versions (< v{0}) may not support all certificate trust scenarios.
trans-unit.target: Older certificate versions (< v{0}) may not support all certificate trust scenarios.

trans-unit.source: HTTPS development certificate has an older version ({0})
trans-unit.target: HTTPS development certificate has an older version ({0})

trans-unit.source: The certificate is in the trusted store, but SSL_CERT_DIR is not configured to include '{0}'. Some applications may not trust the certificate. 'aspire run' will configure this automatically.
trans-unit.target: The certificate is in the trusted store, but SSL_CERT_DIR is not configured to include '{0}'. Some applications may not trust the certificate. 'aspire run' will configure this automatically.

trans-unit.source: Set SSL_CERT_DIR in your shell profile: {0}
trans-unit.target: Set SSL_CERT_DIR in your shell profile: {0}

trans-unit.source: HTTPS development certificate is only partially trusted
trans-unit.target: HTTPS development certificate is only partially trusted

trans-unit.source: Run '{0}' to trust the HTTPS development certificate.
trans-unit.target: Run '{0}' to trust the HTTPS development certificate.
trans-unit.note: {0} is the aspire certs trust command (not localizable)

trans-unit.source: Install discovery failed. See the Aspire CLI logs for details.
trans-unit.target: Install discovery failed. See the Aspire CLI logs for details.
輸出結果為 JSON 以用於工具整合
摘要: {0} 個通過、{1} 個警告、{2} 個已失敗

trans-unit.note: Suffix appended to the running CLI's path so the user can identify which install is themselves.
Placeholder shown in the table when an install was found but not asked to self-describe (e.g., trust gate).
Shown when an install is the first Aspire CLI resolved from PATH.
Shown when an install was not discovered from PATH.
Shown when an install is on PATH but an earlier Aspire CLI entry shadows it.
Placeholder shown in the table when an install was probed but did not return usable data.
Placeholder shown in the table when a field could not be determined for a discovered install.
